text:
To determine how many pencils were left at the end of the year, we can break the problem into a few steps.

1. Calculate the initial total number of pencils in the class.
   Marissa's class has 30 students, and each student started with 10 pencils.
   \[
   \text{Initial total number of pencils} = 30 \times 10 = 300
   \]

2. Determine the number of pencils used after two months.
   After two months, 1/5 of the total pencils were used.
   \[
   \text{Pencils used after two months} = \frac{1}{5} \times 300 = 60
   \]
   The number of pencils remaining after two months is:
   \[
   \text{Pencils remaining after two months} = 300 - 60 = 240
   \]

3. Calculate the number of pencils remaining at the end of the year.
   At the end of the year, only 1/3 of the remaining pencils were left.
   \[
   \text{Pencils remaining at the end of the year} = \frac{1}{3} \times 240 = 80
   \]

So, the number of pencils left at the end of the year is \(\boxed{80}\).
